media_attachments.description:
Martin en Reyes zien in de NWT echter een problematische klassendimensie. Studenten ontvangen 2200 euro per maand, een enorm hoog bedrag voor een stage en daarmee een grote prikkel voor wie financieel kwetsbaarder is. Martin: ‘Dit is de realiteit in de VS, geen hellend vlak, maar werkelijkheid. Militaire recruters op campussen richten zich op studenten in armoede om kanonnenvoer te worden.’

Het hoge salaris is ook voor Wolbers een dilemma geweest bij het aangaan van de samenwerking. ‘Dit soort opleidingen binnen Defensie, daar hoort een veiligheidsscreening bij, en daarnaast een psychologische en fysieke keuring. Dat kan eigenlijk alleen als je ook in dienst komt bij Defensie voor die periode. En daar krijg je inderdaad ook voor betaald.’

Ondanks dat soort overwegingen voelen Reyes en Martin zich ‘kikkertjes in langzaam kokend water’. De Nationale Weerbaarheidstraining is volgens hen onderdeel van een grotere beweging, waarvan ze bang zijn dat die hen langzaam maar zeker zal verstrikken in een structuur waarin zij indirect bijdragen aan bloedvergieten.

Hun angstbeeld toont sterke overeenkomsten met wat de Israëlische onderzoeker Maya Wind beschrijft in Towers of Ivory and Steel (2024). Wind laat zien hoe door de verwevenheid van de Israëlische universiteiten met de idf, het Israëlische leger, academisch onderzoek en onderwijs gebruikt worden voor de ontwikkeling, uitvoering en legitimering van militair geweld. Dat betekent onder andere dat de academische programma’s ‘Israëlische soldaten opleiden voordat ze worden uitgezonden’.

content:
US officials say the real cost of the Iran war is about $40–50 billion, nearly double the Pentagon’s public estimate of $25 billion.

The Pentagon’s figure excludes some costs, including damaged or destroyed equipment and military installations.

A major portion of the cost comes from replacing used munitions, including expensive systems like MQ-9 Reaper drones.

Additional hidden expenses include fuel, deployments, base operations, and other military logistics.

The war is also contributing to higher fuel and food prices for US consumers, with some estimates suggesting about $150 extra per household monthly.

#today Went to see my beloved GP of 20 years. He took my blood for the first time for us.

Had the best trick. You know the strap they put on your arm above where they're taking the blood from?

After they take the needle out and put the cotton wad/tape on, get them to put the strap with the same pressure *over* the wad while they finish up their paperwork.

It means that tape/wad is going to be super secure, you're not holding it, bleeding will be over faster too. Genius.

content:
#LetterboxdFriday

In 1946, in a 'Where were we before we were so rudely interrupted?'' gesture, 11 films tied for the top prize at the Cannes Film Festival. These are 4 of those films.

ANTOINE & ANTOINETTE - 🇫🇷

A working class couple win the lottery and lose the ticket. Masterfully directed. Comfortably the best film of these four

⭐ ⭐ ⭐ ⭐

PASTORAL SYMPHONY - 🇫🇷

A pastor takes in a blind orphan, falls in love with her when she grows up... and his love rival is his own son. So... ick.

My favourite moment is when it suddenly occurs to them (after she's a fully grown adult) that a surgeon might be able to fix her eyesight. And he does. All kinds of stupid, but entertaining

⭐ ⭐ ⭐

MARIA CANDELARIA - 🇲🇽

The intentional artificiality is nice -- almost Powell & Pressburger level -- but it's VERY melodramatic even for the 40s

⭐ ⭐ ⭐

MEN WITHOUT WINGS - 🇨🇿 🇸🇰 [Actually Czechoslovakia]

Solid Czech Resistance versus Nazis thriller

⭐ ⭐ ⭐

#LastFourWatched

card.title: Meta says it could shut down social media platforms in New Mexico
card.description: Meta is raising the prospect of shutting down social media services in New Mexico in response to a push by state prosecutors for fundamental changes to platforms, including Instagram, to protect the mental health and safety of children. The possibility emerged Thursday amid legal gamesmanship in the runup to a bench trial next week on allegations that Meta poses a public nuisance. In a court filing and public statement, Meta said it was unfeasible for the company to meet proposed requirements. Prosecutors are seeking a series of changes to social media platforms for child accounts aimed at reining in compulsive use and preventing child sexual exploitation.

content:
A common delusion among tech-savvy people is that we will continue to be tech-savvy even when we are very old.

We won't. We _might_ not be completely helpless in a few specific fields related to our core interests, but we will _not_ be able to keep up with the latest advances, user interfaces, IT security measures, and so forth.

So if you don't have any kids of your own who are willing to assist you, make sure you stay friends a couple of young people who might be willing to help you out, mkay?

#ParentalTechStupport

card.title: Late Pleistocene pelagic gastropods of southern Taiwan: paleobiodiversity, first fossil records, and regional affinity
card.description: Holoplanktonic gastropods (pteropods and heteropods) are major components of modern Indo–West Pacific (IWP) plankton, yet their fossil record in this region remains sparse. Expanding the spatial and temporal coverage of fossil data is essential for reconstructing dispersal pathways of pelagic fauna within the IWP and understanding the origins of present-day diversity. Here, we describe a newly discovered Late Pleistocene assemblage of pelagic gastropods from southern Taiwan. The assemblage comprises 14 pteropod and eight heteropod taxa, most representing the first fossil records of holoplanktonic gastropods from Taiwan. We also evaluate variation in paleobiodiversity between depositional environments of the Szekou Formation. Species richness and density do not differ significantly between restricted and open lagoon settings, contrasting with patterns reported for benthic bivalves. To place these findings in a broader context, the newly reported assemblages were analyzed together with other Pleistocene assemblages across the IWP region. Only weak geographic and temporal separation was detected, suggesting a relatively cosmopolitan community composition in subtropical waters during the Pleistocene, likely reflecting low temperature variability despite glacial–interglacial cycles. Indicator species analysis further suggests a later arrival at higher latitudes for the pteropods Telodiacria quadridentata and Heliconoides inflata, which show associations with late Pleistocene sites, whereas Styliola subula displays a distribution resembling its modern range, being most closely associated with assemblages from Taiwan and southern Japan.

Il Fatto Quotidiano: Oroscopo di maggio: “Toro, il 16 ci sarà un nuovo inizio personale. Acquario, arrivano cambiamenti improvvisi ma costruttivi. Pesci? Serve chiarezza”

Maggio si apre con un novilunio in Scorpione che porta a galla emozioni profonde e processi di trasformazione già in atto. È un mese che alterna bisogno di stabilità e desiderio di cambiamento, con due momenti lunari importanti: la Luna Nuova del 16 in Toro, che richiama concretezza e radicamento, e la Luna Piena del 31 in Sagittario, che apre prospettive più ampie e invita a guardare oltre i limiti attuali.
Mercurio entra in Toro il 3, favorendo riflessioni pratiche e decisioni più lente ma solide, per poi passare in Gemelli il 17, accelerando comunicazioni e scambi. Il 21 il Sole entra in Gemelli, portando leggerezza, movimento e nuove connessioni. Venere il 19 si sposta in Cancro, accentuando il bisogno di protezione emotiva e sincerità nei rapporti, mentre Marte nello stesso giorno passa in Toro, rendendo l’azione più concreta e determinata. Urano in Gemelli spinge verso cambiamenti improvvisi e innovazioni nel modo di pensare e comunicare.
L'articolo Oroscopo di maggio: “Toro, il 16 ci sarà un nuovo inizio personale. Acquario, arrivano cambiamenti improvvisi ma costruttivi. Pesci? Serve chiarezza” proviene da Il Fatto Quotidiano.

May Horoscope: “Taurus, the 16th will be a new personal beginning. Aquarius, sudden but constructive changes are arriving. Pisces? Clarity is needed.”

May opens with a new moon in Scorpio, bringing to the surface deep emotions and transformation processes already in progress. It’s a month that alternates between a need for stability and a desire for change, with two important lunar events: the New Moon on the 16th in Taurus, which calls for concreteness and grounding, and the Full Moon on the 31st in Sagittarius, which opens up broader perspectives and invites us to look beyond current limitations.

Mercury enters Taurus on the 3rd, favoring practical reflections and more deliberate but solid decisions, before moving to Gemini on the 17th, accelerating communications and exchanges. On the 21st, the Sun enters Gemini, bringing lightness, movement, and new connections. Venus moves to Cancer on the 19th, intensifying the need for emotional protection and sincerity in relationships, while Mars passes to Taurus on the same day, making action more concrete and determined. card.title: #dataengineering #tech #humeur #mvp #agile | Gabriel C.
card.description:
🎯 "Arrêtez de chercher le projet data parfait. Voici pourquoi l’imperfection est une force"

En data engineering, **trop de projets échouent** parce qu’on cherche la **solution parfaite**. Voici pourquoi **l’imperfection est une force** :

🔹 **1. Le "parfait" est l’ennemi du "bon"** :
   - *Exemple* : Un client a passé **1 an** à concevoir un data lake "parfait"… avant de réaliser qu’un **simple rapport SQL** suffisait.
   - **Solution** : **Livrez un MVP en 3 mois**, puis itérez.

🔹 **2. Les données sont toujours sales** :
   - *"On nettoiera plus tard."* → **Spoiler** : "Plus tard" n’arrive jamais.
   - **Solution** : **Intégrez le nettoyage dans le pipeline** dès le début.

🔹 **3. Les besoins changent** :
   - *Exemple* : Un projet "Big Data" est devenu obsolète avant même d’être fini parce que les besoins métiers avaient évolué.
   - **Solution** : **Revoir les objectifs tous les 3 mois**.

💡 **Notre approche** :
- **80/20** : 80% des besoins sont couverts par 20% des fonctionnalités.
- **Itération** : Améliorez au fur et à mesure, ne visez pas la perfection.
- **Feedback utilisateurs** : Impliquez-les **dès le début**.

💬 **Quel projet "parfait" avez-vous vu échouer ?**

card.image_description: Graph from NetBlocks showing network connectivity in Iran from 24 February 2026 to 1 May 2026. The y-axis represents normalized connectivity, ranging from 0% to 100%, and the x-axis represents the dates. The green line representing Iran's connectivity is high through the initial time period, with a sharp drop on the morning of 28 February. The continued drop in connectivity aligns with a nation-scale internet blackout imposed by authorities after joint military strikes by the US and Israel. A brief restoration was observed on 18 March. The minimum and current connectivity levels are indicated as 1% and 1%, respectively. The chart has a dark background with a red horizontal arrow labeled 'SHUTDOWN' indicating the disruption period, and the NetBlocks logo in the lower left corner with the Mapping Internet Freedom slogan.
